# Key Magic 0.1.1

Key Magic 0.1.1 is a focused stability release for the portable Windows build. It fixes a release-only packaging defect that could prevent the bundled dashboard from starting correctly when `KeyMagic.exe` was run outside the source tree, including downloaded GitHub artifacts.

## What Changed In 0.1.1

- Fixed the embedded dashboard packaging for the portable single-file release so the bundled UI resolves correctly from inside `KeyMagic.exe`.
- Added startup diagnostics that record bootstrap progress and fatal startup exceptions to `%APPDATA%\KeyMagic\logs\startup.log`.
- Hardened runtime startup so Key Magic can continue launching the tray host even if the local dashboard fails to initialize for a session.
- Updated the release workflows to use Node 24-compatible `actions/github-script@v8`.

## Why This Release Matters

- `v0.1.0` could work from a local repository publish while still failing when the same app was run from a downloaded portable artifact.
- That mismatch was caused by a missing embedded file manifest required by `ManifestEmbeddedFileProvider`.
- `v0.1.1` restores the intended portable release behavior: the release executable can run correctly outside the repository and serve the bundled local dashboard from embedded assets.

## What Ships In This Release

- `KeyMagic.exe`: a portable, self-contained single-file Windows application.
- `KeyMagic.exe.sha256`: a checksum file for download verification.
- Embedded dashboard assets and Key Magic branding bundled inside the executable.
- Local configuration stored at `%APPDATA%\KeyMagic\config.json`.
- Startup diagnostics written to `%APPDATA%\KeyMagic\logs\startup.log`.

## Operational Notes

- No configuration migration is required from `v0.1.0`.
- The dashboard remains local-only and listens on `http://localhost:5199` by default.
- If the dashboard is unavailable during startup, the tray app can still start and the reason is recorded in the startup log.
- Some Windows-reserved shortcuts still cannot be intercepted from user mode.

## Release Trust And Delivery

- Stable releases continue to be rebuilt from a validated `main` commit rather than published directly from unreviewed branch state.
- The release flow publishes a SHA-256 checksum alongside the executable.
- Authenticode signing and GitHub provenance attestations remain supported when repository secrets and platform support are available.

## Packaging Notes

- This release is still delivered as a portable executable, not an installer or MSIX package.
- `KeyMagic.Tester` remains in the repository for diagnostics and development validation, but it is not part of the shipped stable asset set.
- The intended public download experience remains a single executable plus checksum.
